title: anginon ternatum
intro: Anginon ternatum, also known as Frasera speciosa or Indian paintbrush, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to North America. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues such as diarrhea and ulcers. The roots and leaves of Anginon ternatum contain iridoid glycosides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing fever, and soothing respiratory issues. Anginon ternatum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
